taskDescription: Drag the correct anatomical terms into the blanks to complete these statements about the arm and cubital fossa.
textField:
The arm is divided into anterior and posterior compartments by the medial and lateral *intermuscular* septa.

The anterior compartment of the arm contains three muscles: *biceps* brachii, *brachialis*, and coracobrachialis.

Saturday night paralysis is caused by compression of the *radial* nerve as it runs in the *spiral groove* of the humerus.

The boundaries of the cubital fossa are: laterally the *brachioradialis*, medially the *pronator teres*, and superiorly an imaginary line between the epicondyles.

The median cubital vein connects the *cephalic* and *basilic* veins and is the preferred site for venipuncture.

The *musculocutaneous* nerve pierces coracobrachialis, supplies the anterior compartment muscles, and continues as the lateral cutaneous nerve of the forearm.

The *brachial* artery is the main arterial supply of the arm and divides at the cubital fossa into the radial and *ulnar* arteries.

overallFeedback.feedback: Review the arm and cubital fossa anatomy and try again.
Good effort! Review the areas you missed.
